section: NEW BOILING SALT FAST BREEDER REACTOR CONCEPTS *
text: Some preliminary calculations for 1000 MwT reactors of both types were performed.

section: NEW BOILING SALT FAST BREEDER REACTOR CONCEPTS *
text: The characteristics considered are: SAWA concept: 6000 litre core, 0.33 MW/l, cooled with $1340\mathrm{kg}$ of $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ per second; WARS concept: 10500 litre core, 0.40 MW/l, cooled with $3300\mathrm{kg}$ of $\mathrm{Hg}$ per second.

section: 1. LIQUID FUELS FOR FAST BREEDER REACTORS
text: The increasing interest in fast breeder reactors [10] is bound to cause fused salt fuels to receive still more attention [11-13].
The cooling system is a decisive element in reactor design, especially in fast breeders [14].
Recently the interesting idea of a direct contact metallic coolant-metallic fuel immiscible system was presented by Hammond and Humphreys [15].
For fused chloride fuel for fast breeders the concept was discussed by Alexander [11], and recent remarks were made by Moore and Fawcett [16] and Killingback [17].
have been the object of investigations in this laboratory since 1960 [18-21].
Two reactor concepts will be considered: the SAWA concept [22, 23] and the WARS concept [24, 25].

It must be noticed that the vapour pressure of the coolants (AlCl₃ and Hgmet) at 750-800°C is by about six to seven orders of magnitude higher than the vapour pressure of plutonium and uranium chlorides, which assures that the part of the cooling system which contains vapour can not reach nuclear criticality.
Besides the data given in table 1 a number of features may be ascribed to the proposed reactor concepts which one can consider as advantages.

The volumetric thermal expansion coefficient is $3 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{OC}^{-1}$ and the corresponding decrease in reactivity would be about $1.5\%$ per $10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ temperature increase.
To avoid oscillation of the fuel system due to high instability of the reactivity, an appropriate geometry for the system in the region of the free boiling surface would be applied [29].
